svg.text: Player attribute controlling character attributes, skills, and progression

g.text: Class is a player attribute that primarily affects the player's character. It determines the
initial attributes and skills of most player entities and character entities.
Classes differentiate player gameplay roles: abilities, character skills, and development paths.
Core Function: Coordinates and encapsulates multiple modules — packages a complete set of
configurations and uniformly modifies both player and character on class switch.
Except for class level, other data is overwritten only once during class switching.
Creators can still use Node Graphs to modify player and character data afterward.
Example: When Class 1 is activated, Normal Attack is replaced with Skill 1 by default.
You can still use Node Graphs to swap Skill 1 with other skills later.
Using "Initialize Character Skills" restores skills to those configured in the class template.
Entry Point:
Combat Preset > Class tab > Click "Create Class" > Confirm to create a new class template.
Multiple class templates can be created. Players can switch between them during gameplay.
1. Basic Settings
Select Camera — Camera template applied when player switches to this class.
Select Layout — Main screen layout applied on class switch.
Scan Tag Recognition Rules — Filters active units from scannable objects:
Distance From Center of FOV — Closer to screen center = higher priority
Actual Position Distance — Closer to character in world coords = higher priority
Movement Controls:
Allow Jump
Accepts jump inputs
Allow Sprint
Accepts sprint inputs
Allow Climb
Can climb surfaces
Allow Glide
Deploy wind gliders in air
When climbing is disabled for a class, the character cannot climb even if object collision
is set to climbable. Class settings override object-level climb configuration.
2. Level Settings
Class level is roughly the character's level. Character level syncs with class level.
Level — Character's class level when first switching to this class during gameplay.
Maximum Level — Highest level this class can reach.
Level-up EXP Configuration (two methods):
Linear Formula
EXP = current level x constant 1 + constant 2
List Configuration
Two-dimensional table for precise per-level EXP.
Multi-class Level Persistence: When switching between classes, each class's level is recorded.
Switching back restores the level and experience to their previous values.
3. Basic Battle Attributes
Class attributes directly affect the corresponding player's character. Base combat attributes
cannot be configured on player templates — only through class settings.
Base HP
Default HP without growth stacking
Base ATK
Default ATK without growth stacking
Base DEF
Default DEF without growth stacking
Attribute Growth (3 types):
No Growth
Attributes stay at base values.
Default Growth Curve
Preview only, not modifiable.
Custom Growth Curve
Fully configurable curve.
Growth Formula: Final HP = Base HP x Level HP Multiplier (same for ATK and DEF)
Max Stamina — Maximum stamina value for this class.
Aggro Configuration — If "Custom" aggro type in stage settings, configure aggro multiplier.
4. Unit Status
Unit status added by the class is automatically applied to the character when the player
switches to that class. See Unit Statuses documentation for configuration details.
5. Interrupt Resistance Configuration
Characters have three Hyperarmor-related statuses and an Interrupt Threshold.
A
Interrupt Resistance Status
Interrupt Threshold starts at 0. When hit by interrupt attacks (threshold > 0) not exceeding
the limit, character shows hit reactions only (no knockback/launch). Threshold increases by
incoming attack value (affected by Interrupt Resistance multiplier of current status).
Threshold decays per second at the configured rate until reaching 0.
B
Interrupt-Vulnerable State
Triggered when Interrupt Threshold reaches maximum. Threshold stays at max (no increase/decrease).
When hit, target may be knocked back or launched (depends on attack launch force).
Lasts for configured Interrupt Vulnerability Duration, then transitions to Protected Status.
C
Protected Status
After Interrupt-Vulnerable State ends. Threshold stays at 0 (no increase/decrease).
No knockback or launch when hit. Lasts for configured Protected Duration.
After Protected Status ends, returns to Interrupt Resistance Status (new cycle).
Parameters:
Interrupt Value Threshold — Max interrupt damage before entering Vulnerable State.
Interrupt Intake Decay Rate — Amount of interrupt value decayed per second in Resistance Status.
Interrupt Vulnerability Duration — Duration of Vulnerable State (can be 0).
Protected Duration — Duration of Protected Status (can be 0).
Class-specific skills attached to the class. When players switch to this class, the character's
skills automatically switch to the skills set for that class.
Skills in the class template are default skills. After switching, you can still replace skills
via Node Graphs. "Initialize Character Skills" restores the class template defaults.
When switching to this class, the system automatically applies pre-configured node graphs
to both the player and character. Editing interface is under the Node Graph tab.
Class Node Graphs apply unit support for both players and characters simultaneously.

svg.text: VII. Using Node Graphs to Modify Classes

g.text: Event Nodes:
Class-related event triggers
Execution Nodes:
Modify Class Experience
Modify Player's Class
Query Nodes:
Query Class information
Query Class Experience / Level
